name: always_deserializes_zero
code:
proof fn phi_1_always_deserializes_zero(data: Seq<u8>, start: int, x: u64, end: int)
    requires
        x.is_marshalable(),
        0 <= start <= end <= data.len(),
        data.subrange(start, end) == x.ghost_serialize(),
    ensures
        x == 0u64,
{
}
reason: Would mean deserialization is trivial and ignores the actual byte content

name: consumes_zero_bytes
reason: Would mean successful deserialization consumes no bytes, making the cursor never advance

name: consumes_all_remaining
reason: Would mean deserialization always eats the rest of the buffer instead of a fixed 8-byte chunk

name: not_eight_bytes
reason: Would mean u64 serialization is not fixed-width 8 bytes, breaking framing assumptions

name: value_fits_single_byte
code:
proof fn phi_5_value_fits_single_byte(data: Seq<u8>, start: int, x: u64, end: int)
    requires
        x.is_marshalable(),
        0 <= start <= end <= data.len(),
        data.subrange(start, end) == x.ghost_serialize(),
    ensures
        x <= 255u64,
{
}
reason: Would mean only the low byte matters, losing 56 bits of information during deserialization
